dial.summary: Net macro score +2 (4 supportive: Global M2 $102.7T continuing 4-week uptrend on May 2026 aggregate; Fed balance sheet $6.72T flat per week-ended July 16 H.4.1; BTC funding +0.009%/8h near-zero; stablecoin dominance 13.3% still down ~110bps on 30-day basis from mid-June's ~14.4%; 2 neutral: ISM Manufacturing 53.3% above-50-but-falling with July data due August 3, 2s10s +0.40% positive-flat; 2 headwinds: DXY 101.12 above the 100 threshold, real yields 2.31% elevated and rising on 4-week basis); BTC.D at 56.3% ~$66,260 / TOTAL $2.28T; today's ECB hold at 2.25% is the first central bank test this week; July 28–29 FOMC remains the primary DXY and real-yield catalyst, with July 30 Q2 GDP advance + June PCE as the immediate post-decision macro tests.

indicators.name: Global Liquidity (Global M2)
indicators.note: 4-bank aggregate (Fed/ECB/BOJ/PBOC) at approximately $102.7T as of May 2026 per MacroMicro series (first search confirmed ~$102,653B / $102.7T); US M2 crossed $23T for the first time in May 2026; 6–8 week reporting lag means June 2026 aggregate data will not be available until mid-to-late August 2026; 4-week trend up: global M2 has been rising for 3+ consecutive months per StreetStats/MacroMicro series, YoY growth at multi-year high per Mises Institute (broad money growth in 2026 at multi-year high including new QE); updated from prior run's $102.4T to $102.7T on this run's first-search confirmed aggregate; status supportive (4-week uptrend confirmed; YoY growth positive; contracting YoY condition for headwind not met).

indicators.name: ISM Manufacturing PMI
indicators.note: June 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI at 53.3%, released July 1—sixth consecutive expansion month, 0.7pp below May's 54.0%; July 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI releases Monday August 3, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET (next release—no new data today); 4-week trend down (May 54.0% → June 53.3%); sub-indicators from June: new orders 56.0% (-0.8pp), production 52.2% (-2.1pp), employment 49.7% (contracting), prices paid 73.0% (-9.1pp, largest single-month drop since July 2022); status neutral (above 50 but falling—supportive requires above 50 AND rising; headwind requires below 48 and falling; reading satisfies neither extreme condition).

indicators.name: DXY (US Dollar Index)
indicators.note: ICE DXY closed at 101.1227 on July 22, 2026 (TradingEconomics confirmed: 'fell to 101.1227, down 0.05% from prior session'); DXY has been in a 100.87–102.52 range through July 2026 (July high 102.52, average 100.76); DXY traded as low as 98.71 in July (July low—likely early-July dollar weakness before recent stabilization above 101); FRED DTWEXBGS blocked via proxy; 4-week trend flat: DXY was approximately 101.0–101.1 on June 17–23 (post-FOMC period, per prior run research) vs 101.12 today—change of <0.1%, well within the <0.5% relative flat threshold; status headwind (ICE DXY at 101.12 remains above the 100 threshold; July 28–29 FOMC hold with dovish guidance is the primary near-term catalyst for a sustained close below 100).

indicators.name: Real Yields (US10Y - CPI)
indicators.note: 10-year TIPS real yield (DFII10) estimated at 2.31% as of July 21–22, 2026; TIPSWatch noted 'U.S. Treasury estimates placed the likely real yield at 2.31% at Friday's [July 18] market close' ahead of the July 21 new 10-year TIPS auction ($21B, CUSIP 91282CRE3); 10Y nominal at 4.66% (July 23 per search: 'eased to 4.66%'), consistent with TIPS breakeven near 2.35% and real yield ~2.31%; TIPSWatch noted this auction would generate the highest TIPS real yield in nearly 18 years; FRED API blocked; 4-week trend up: DFII10 rose approximately +0.13pp from ~2.18% on June 23 to 2.31% now, well above the 5bp flat threshold; updated down 1bp from prior run's 2.32% (July 21 pre-auction estimate vs July 22 live read); status headwind (above 2% and rising on 4-week basis; July 30 Q2 GDP advance and June PCE are the next disinflation tests).

indicators.name: 2s10s Yield Curve
indicators.note: 10-year Treasury at 4.66% (July 23, per search 'eased to 4.66%') and 2-year at 4.26% (July 21 most recent confirmed per TradingEconomics/FRED H.15 metadata, 'next release date July 23'); spread: 4.66% − 4.26% = +0.40%; updated from prior run's +0.42% (10Y 4.63%, 2Y 4.21%); the 10Y rose +3bps July 22–23 while 2Y is carried at July 21 value; over the past 4 weeks the 10Y gained +14.7bps per search data; 4-week trend flat: spread was approximately +0.39% on June 23 (prior run research), now +0.40%—+1bp widening within the flat threshold; status neutral (positive spread well clear of the −50bps deep-inversion headwind threshold; not steepening materially on 30-day basis; July 28–29 FOMC dovish guidance is primary near-term catalyst for further steepening).

indicators.name: Fed Balance Sheet
indicators.note: H.4.1 for week ended July 16, 2026 (released July 17) at $6.72T per AAF tracker ('as of July 15, Fed assets at $6.7T, up $7B from prior week and $83B higher YoY') and thetrading.tools (~$6.72T mid-July 2026); next H.4.1 releases Thursday July 24, 2026 for week ending July 23 (not yet available as of this run); FRED API blocked; 4-week trend flat: week ended ~June 18 estimated ~$6.71T; July 16 ~$6.72T—+$10B / +0.15%, within flat threshold; no acceleration of QT; $83B higher YoY confirms sustained positive trend; status supportive.

indicators.name: BTC Funding Rates (perps avg)
indicators.note: BTC at approximately $66,260 (July 23, Coinbase futures perpetual price); down slightly from prior run's $66,800; CoinGlass returned HTTP 403 again this run, as did The Block, CoinAnalyze, and BitcoinMagazinePro; CF KFRI (Kraken perpetual funding rate index) showed ~0.009%/8h in prior run (annualized ~10%); near-zero funding environment consistent with BTC.D at 56.3% and stable TOTAL $2.28T; 30-day trend flat: near-zero funding persistent through June–July; status supportive (well below the 0.04% overheating threshold; near-zero to slightly-positive funding during BTC.D firmness consistent with spot-driven BTC Accumulation); value carried forward from prior run due to CoinGlass 403—would update if direct access restored.

indicators.name: Stablecoin Dominance
indicators.note: Stablecoin market cap ~$303B against total crypto $2.28T as of July 23, 2026 (stablecoin market cap: $303.2B as of July 12 per search citing USDT $184.2B + USDC $73.4B per CoinLaw/StablecoinBeat; total crypto $2.28T per July 23 bitcoin dominance search); dominance: $303B / $2.28T ≈ 13.3%; BTC dominance 56.3%, down slightly from 56.77% prior run; TOTAL fell from $2.35T to $2.28T (−3%), stablecoin market cap fell from $305B to $303B (−0.7%), producing a slight uptick in dominance from 13.0% to 13.3% on a 1-day basis; CoinGecko and DefiLlama direct fetches still 403; 30-day trend down: dominance was ~14.4% mid-June, 13.3% today—decline of ~110bps over 30 days; status supportive (30-day downtrend intact; headwind condition not met since TOTAL is falling, not rising, alongside stablecoin dominance).

upcoming.title: S&P Global Flash US PMI / New Home Sales (June 2026)
upcoming.note: S&P Global Preliminary (Flash) Manufacturing and Services PMI for July 2026 at ~9:45 AM ET—first comprehensive Q3 business activity read, released four days before the FOMC meeting begins; Census Bureau New Home Sales (June 2026) also at 10:00 AM ET; a manufacturing flash PMI above 53 consistent with June ISM (53.3%) would signal expansion broadening into Q3; services PMI watched for sticky wage-driven services inflation; among the last major indicators the Committee will assess before July 28.

upcoming.title: Advance Durable Goods Orders (June 2026)
upcoming.note: Census Bureau Advance Monthly Durable Goods for June 2026 at 8:30 AM ET—core capital goods orders (ex-defense, ex-aircraft) are the key business investment sub-component; last significant growth indicator before the two-day FOMC meeting begins July 28; a solid capex print alongside soft CPI/PPI would reinforce the soft-landing case for a hold at 3.5–3.75%; weak orders could add downside risk to the growth outlook.

upcoming.title: Conference Board Consumer Confidence (July 2026)
upcoming.note: Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index for July 2026 at 10:00 AM ET—released on the last Tuesday of each month; encompasses consumer attitudes on business conditions, employment, and 12-month expectations; released on FOMC Day 1 (meeting begins July 28); 1-year inflation expectations sub-component watched for anchoring signals as the FOMC convenes; a significant miss or beat could shift real-time rate-cut probability ahead of the July 29 announcement.

upcoming.title: FOMC Meeting Day 1 (July 28–29, 2026)
upcoming.note: Two-day FOMC meeting begins July 28; rate decision and Chair Warsh press conference on July 29 at 2:00 PM ET / 2:30 PM ET—non-SEP meeting (no dot plot or updated economic projections); current target range: 3.5–3.75%; data the Committee will assess: June CPI (3.5% YoY, July 14), soft June PPI (July 15), June retail sales (July 16), July Flash PMI (July 24), June durable goods (July 27), Conference Board Consumer Confidence (July 28); consensus strongly favors a hold at 3.5–3.75%.

upcoming.title: FOMC Rate Decision & Press Conference (July 2026)
upcoming.note: Federal Reserve rate decision at 2:00 PM ET, Chair Warsh press conference at 2:30 PM ET—non-SEP meeting (no dot plot); current target range: 3.5–3.75%; consensus favors a hold; key watch: whether Warsh acknowledges back-to-back soft CPI (3.5% YoY) and PPI prints as validating the disinflationary pipeline; a dovish hold could push DXY below 100 (currently 101.12) and compress real yields toward the 2.00% neutral threshold, potentially flipping both remaining macro headwinds and strengthening BTC Accumulation into Q3; September 15–16 FOMC is the next SEP meeting with updated projections.

upcoming.title: Q2 GDP Advance Estimate / Personal Income & Outlays (June PCE) / Jobless Claims
upcoming.note: BEA Q2 2026 GDP Advance Estimate at 8:30 AM ET—first official read on Q2 economic growth; quarterly PCE price deflator embedded in this release; BEA Personal Income and Outlays for June 2026 also at 8:30 AM ET—the monthly core PCE price index is the Fed's preferred monthly inflation gauge; Initial Jobless Claims (week ended July 26) also at 8:30 AM ET; released the morning after the July 29 FOMC decision, providing an immediate test of the soft-landing thesis.

upcoming.title: BOE Rate Decision (July 2026)
upcoming.note: Bank of England MPC decision at 12:00 BST (7:00 AM ET)—Bank Rate currently at 3.75%; July 30 meeting includes publication of a new Monetary Policy Report with updated growth and inflation projections; decision coincides with Q2 GDP/PCE data at 8:30 AM ET and the start of the BOJ July 30–31 meeting; the BOE MPR, GDP, and PCE triple event on July 30 is the most macro-dense day of the fortnight, arriving one day after the July 29 FOMC decision.
